The search successfully returned the exact paper I queried: "WILDS: A Benchmark of in-the-Wild Distribution Shifts" (Koh et al., ICML 2020). This is a seminal work that perfectly addresses the need for a foundational citation on real-world deep learning pitfalls. It documents how models trained on standard datasets fail when deployed in environments with natural distribution shifts (e.g., different hospitals, cameras, demographics, or time periods). I will select this paper and update the description to explicitly note its relevance to summarizing deployment failure literature, crediting dataset creators, highlighting evaluation gaps, and supporting the paper's core arguments about the lab-to-real performance gap. It will be primarily cited in the Introduction and Background sections.
